ai_agent.role: Central point of communication between Lakeshore and clients — from first contact (pre-sale) through house delivery. Manages leads, upgrades, cost communication, and portal monitoring.

staff.name: Juliana Theodoro
staff.role: Client Relations Leader — main client contact, HubSpot phone access, upgrade processing, client hold communication

staff.name: Daniela Thuaslar
staff.role: Client Relations — client communication, Daily Log monitoring, portal updates

staff.name: Vitoria Moura
staff.role: Client Relations — client communication, status tracking, document coordination

core_knowledge.description: Lead lifecycle: new → contacted → qualified → proposal → negotiation → won/lost. Tracked in HubSpot (still implementing). Each stage has defined actions and SLAs.

Client requests upgrade via WhatsApp → formalize by email → route internally for quote → return quote to client → if approved, register in Daily Log fixed card → monitor execution. Common upgrades: paint palette, ceiling fan, washer/dryer, extra cabinet, exterior patio.

When costs exceed budget by >$1,000, CR receives notification from Utilities/Permits/other departments, prepares cost breakdown for client, obtains approval or rejection, and notifies Financeiro for processing.
Four hold types: Client Hold (financing/design change), Permit Hold (county approval), Financial Hold (draw/payment), Technical Hold (engineering/inspection). Each has distinct escalation chains.

Three options: Waiver (client declines), Private Inspector (third-party signs), Third Party Authorization. Checklist covers 45 items across General, Electric, AC, Plumbing, Doors/Windows, and Landscaping categories.

CO within 1 year of permit+slab. Change orders must be written and signed. 1-year builder warranty from CO. Allowances not used are NOT reimbursed. Dispute resolution: negotiation → mediation → binding arbitration.

daily_workflows.name: Daily Client Communication
steps: Check HubSpot for incoming client messages (WhatsApp, calls, email)
Identify request type: status update, contract question, upgrade, complaint
Search Daily Log in BuilderTrend for project status
If insufficient: check contract spreadsheet or meeting notes
If still missing: consult Scheduling or field supervisors
Process RCRC channel requests from other departments
critical_decisions: Prioritizing urgent client concerns vs routine updates

daily_workflows.name: Upgrade Management
steps: Receive upgrade request from client via WhatsApp
Request client to formalize via email
Route internally for quotation to responsible departments
If approved: register in Daily Log fixed card
critical_decisions: Whether upgrade removes house from production line
Ensuring sub executes the approved upgrade

daily_workflows.name: Portal Monitoring
steps: Check BuilderTrend client portal for inconsistencies
Verify schedule, budget, photos, invoices, documents
When client questions an inconsistency, explain and seek correction
critical_decisions: Whether to proactively fix portal info vs wait for client complaint

features_used: Exceeding Costs tab — budget threshold events for utility services

handoffs.trigger: Extra cost identified (extension, nitrogen, well, underground electric >$1K over budget)
handoffs.data_exchanged: Cost details, utility type, house ID, documentation
handoffs.impact: Client must approve before construction can proceed — blocks pipeline

handoffs.data_exchanged: Approved budget, design specs, contract terms
handoffs.impact: Without budget approval, contract cannot be signed

handoffs.data_exchanged: Approval confirmation, cost amount, payment terms
Client info, closing date, walkthrough results, punch list
handoffs.impact: Warranty period begins; CR provides lawn cut authorizations for closed houses

pain_points.description: HubSpot still in implementation phase — only Juliana has phone access (user limitation)
pain_points.current_workaround: Other team members use WhatsApp and email directly
pain_points.proposed_solution: Expand HubSpot licenses to full team

pain_points.description: BuilderTrend client portal is dysfunctional — schedule never worked, budget shows 'price adjustment' that scares clients, items not marked complete after payment
pain_points.current_workaround: Team constantly explains portal inconsistencies to clients
pain_points.proposed_solution: Custom client portal with accurate real-time data or fix BT portal configuration

pain_points.description: Upgrades disappear in the process — sub forgets to execute, nobody confirms completion, fixed card not monitored
pain_points.proposed_solution: Upgrade tracking system with automated reminders and completion verification

pain_points.description: Slow information retrieval — must search Daily Logs, spreadsheets, and contact multiple departments to answer a single client question
pain_points.proposed_solution: Unified project status dashboard accessible to CR

tribal_knowledge.description: Juliana knows which clients need proactive updates, which prefer minimal contact, and which are high-risk for disputes. This relationship context is not documented.
The team knows informal channels to expedite hold resolution — who to call at which department, when to escalate to Samuel Torres vs Leo.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: Holds take longer to resolve; client frustration increases

training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Hold types, escalation levels, communication channel rules
Upgrade flow steps, threshold rules, approval documentation
Contract terms, walkthrough options, warranty start date rules

compliance_requirements.requirement: Change orders must be written documents signed by both parties — oral changes are invalid
compliance_requirements.verification: CR ensures all upgrade approvals are formalized in writing

compliance_requirements.requirement: Client must receive written notification of excluded cosmetic items

key_metrics.target: 100% of approved upgrades executed before closing
key_metrics.measurement: Fixed card audit at house closing
